candidate_problems.text: Which interventions most effectively decouple the flow of antibiotic use from the flow of resistance selection at system scale: narrow diagnostics, stewardship, infection prevention, agricultural restrictions, or microbiome-preserving therapies?
How should reimbursement and regulatory systems be redesigned so that preserving antibiotic effectiveness is rewarded more strongly than maximizing sales volume?
In which infectious contexts does the inherited 'mass chemical suppression' architecture remain locally optimal despite its long-run resistance costs, and why?
